prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response:
Arrr! Listen well, ye scallywag, whilst I spin ye the tale o’ the blue sky above!

’Tis the work o’ the sun’s own fury, I tell ye! When that golden orb climbs the heavens, it sends forth rays fiercer than a kraken’s glare. But the sky—aye, the very air we breathe—is full o’ tiny sea-sprites, invisible to the eye, what sailors call “molecules.” These wee beasties love to scatter the sun’s light, but they favor the blue part o’ the spectrum like a rum-soaked deckhand favors grog!

Blue light, bein’ shorter and spryer than the rest, gets tossed ‘round everywhich way by them air-sprites, dancin’ and flashin’ across the whole blasted dome o’ the sky. So when ye squint up from the crow’s nest, what do ye see? Blue—everywhere! ‘Tis not the sky’s true color, mind ye, but the echo o’ the sun playin’ tricks with them tiny devils in the breeze.

Now quit yer starin’ aloft like a barnacle-brained lubber and swab the deck—or I’ll have yer hide for sailcloth!
